scope: Extend extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:3934) to descend through nested constructor / call / instance / binary nodes when the first arg-list named child cannot be resolved as a direct identifier. Per-language descend-through set: Java { object_creation_expression, method_invocation, parenthesized_expression }; Scala { call_expression, instance_expression, infix_expression }; Cpp { call_expression, binary_expression } CONDITIONAL on M1 tree-sitter parse-shape resolution. BFS-over-named-descendants with bounded recursion (depth 5) and string-kind filter at every level. Closes vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B carry-forwards (3 tests minimum; 2 if cpp Hypothesis A confirmed at M1 → cpp deferred to follow-on milestone cpp-deser-declaration-v1). NO public API change; NO new VulnType/TaintSinkType/TaintSourceType variants; NO new bank entries. The sub-helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent mirrors the BFS pattern already used for PHP echo_statement (taint.rs:3954-3982) and OCaml application_expression (taint.rs:3989-4016).
closes_carry_forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B (cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive — 3 tests; minimum 2 if cpp Hypothesis A confirmed)
enables_followons: cpp-deser-declaration-v1 (CONDITIONAL — only if M1 resolves Hypothesis A; closes 1 cpp_deserialization_positive)
expected_post_milestone_red_count_note: Drops vuln_migration_v1_red red count from 8 to 5 (closes 3 Bucket B). If cpp deferred under Hypothesis A: drops to 6 (closes 2 of 3); 1 cpp carry-forward documented at M3 with follow-on milestone name.

validator_mandates.m1_cpp_shape_resolution_required: M1 MUST resolve tree-sitter-cpp parse-shape Hypothesis A vs B for the fixture line `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);` BEFORE M2 implementation. Document in reports/M1-cpp-ast-shape.txt. Hypothesis A = declaration with function_declarator (helper extension does NOT close cpp); Hypothesis B = call_expression with binary_expression first arg (helper extension closes cpp). If A: cpp scope drops to deferred follow-on milestone cpp-deser-declaration-v1; M2 scope = 2/3 (java + scala only).
validator_mandates.m1_green_baseline_required: M1 MUST capture 158-test GREEN baseline of vuln_migration_v1_red at HEAD 5d46628. Document in reports/M1-green-baseline.txt. M3 compares against this baseline for GREEN→RED regression detection.
validator_mandates.m1_red_capture_required: M1 MUST run the 3 RED fixtures (cpp/java/scala_deserialization_positive) at HEAD 5d46628 and confirm all 3 are RED pre-fix. Document in reports/M1-red-capture.txt.
validator_mandates.m1_no_residual_test_code: M1's cpp parse-shape inspection (if implemented as a transient #[test] in taint_tests.rs) MUST be reverted before M2 commits. Verification: `git diff crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs` returns empty between M1 commit and M2 commit.
validator_mandates.m2_single_file_only: M2 MUST modify ONLY crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs. NO test files, NO other source files, NO Cargo.toml changes.
validator_mandates.m2_no_public_api_change: The new sub-helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent MUST be private (no `pub`). The existing helper signature MUST NOT change.
validator_mandates.m2_no_new_variants: M2 MUST NOT add new VulnType, TaintSinkType, or TaintSourceType variants.
validator_mandates.m2_no_call_node_kinds_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify call_node_kinds() in ast_utils.rs. The descend-through set is internal to the helper and language-specific.
validator_mandates.m2_no_extract_call_name_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify extract_call_name_* per-language helpers in ast_utils.rs.
validator_mandates.m2_no_member_patterns_match_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify member_patterns_match in taint.rs:3845.
validator_mandates.m2_string_kind_filter_at_every_level: The recursive descent MUST apply string_node_kinds(language) filter at EVERY recursion level, not just the outermost. Violation regresses closes-#24 (string-literal substring false positives).
validator_mandates.m2_bounded_recursion: The recursive descent MUST be bounded (max depth 5). Unbounded recursion risks pathological performance on deeply-nested templates / generics.
validator_mandates.m3_green_regression_gate: M3 MUST verify that all currently-GREEN tests in vuln_migration_v1_red (158 tests at HEAD), vuln_migration_v1_composite_red (1/1), rr_framework_integ_test (18/18), and test_e2e_in_security_vuln (36/36 test_e2e_*) remain GREEN. Any GREEN→RED transition triggers immediate M2 revert and root-cause investigation.
validator_mandates.m3_binary_smoke_string_literal_fp: M3 MUST run binary smoke (`tldr vuln <fixture>`) on all 18 fixtures matching crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/*/deserialization_string_literal_fp.* and document 0 findings each. Closes-#24 regression-guard.
validator_mandates.m3_red_count_delta_assertion: M3 MUST verify vuln_migration_v1_red red count drops by exactly 3 (Hypothesis B) or 2 (Hypothesis A). Any other delta triggers investigation before M4.
validator_mandates.m4_changelog_no_public_api_note: M4 CHANGELOG MUST cite that NO public API change occurred and NO new TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType / VulnType variants were added (avoid future confusion).
validator_mandates.m4_no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: M4 MUST NOT push to remote, MUST NOT run cargo publish, MUST NOT bump any version. Local tag only.
validator_mandates.cargo_lock_never_staged: Per-commit guard: `git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` if dirty before staging.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!var-extract-nested-constructor-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's reports + the source code edit at M2 + CHANGELOG.md at M4 are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged.

milestones.title: Investigate cpp parse-shape + capture RED fixtures + 158-GREEN baseline

investigations.purpose: Resolve cpp Hypothesis A vs B by inspecting tree-sitter-cpp parse output for fixture line 7
methods: Add transient #[test] in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs that parses the fixture with tree_sitter_cpp::language() and prints the s-expression. Capture output in reports/M1-cpp-ast-shape.txt. REVERT the transient test before M2 commit (validator mandate m1_no_residual_test_code).
Alternative: small Rust REPL snippet using tree_sitter::Parser with tree_sitter_cpp::language() language; print s-expression of root.
Alternative: tree-sitter parse CLI on the fixture if installed locally.
investigations.decision_outcome_A: Hypothesis A (declaration with function_declarator): cpp deferred to follow-on milestone cpp-deser-declaration-v1. M2 scope = java + scala only. Document in reports/M1-cpp-disposition.json with disposition=DEFERRED.
investigations.decision_outcome_B: Hypothesis B (call_expression with binary_expression first arg): cpp included in M2 scope. Document in reports/M1-cpp-disposition.json with disposition=INCLUDED.

stop_thresholds: 3 RED captured at HEAD 5d46628 in reports/M1-red-capture.txt
158-test GREEN baseline of vuln_migration_v1_red captured in reports/M1-green-baseline.txt
cpp Hypothesis A vs B resolved; reports/M1-cpp-ast-shape.txt + reports/M1-cpp-disposition.json written
If transient test code added for cpp inspection: REVERTED before M1 commit (verified by `git diff crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs` empty)

milestones.title: Implement extract_first_identifier_arg_ast nested-constructor descent extension

additions: Add new private fn extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (BFS-over-named-descendants helper) below extract_first_identifier_arg_ast. Bounded recursion (depth 5). Applies string_node_kinds filter at every level. Returns Option<String> matching the existing helper's identifier-validation rules.

In the existing helper's main loop body (L4039-4062), after the direct-identifier extraction attempt fails for a child, check if the child's kind is in a per-language descend-through set; if so, invoke the new sub-helper.

Per-language descend-through set: Java { object_creation_expression, method_invocation, parenthesized_expression }; Scala { call_expression, instance_expression, infix_expression }; Cpp { call_expression, binary_expression } CONDITIONAL on M1 Hypothesis B resolution.

Add VAR-EXTRACT-NESTED-CONSTRUCTOR-V1 doc-comment block citing M1 cpp disposition reference, the BFS-pattern precedent (PHP echo_statement L3954-3982; OCaml application_expression L3989-4016), and the per-language node-kind set rationale.
preserved: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast existing direct-identifier path for non-descend-through first-args (C fgets, Python read, etc.)
string_node_kinds(language) filter at outer arg-list iteration
Both invocation sites at taint.rs:4201 (sources) and :4316 (sinks) — no caller-side change needed

cargo clippy --all-targets --workspace -- -D warnings PASS

Hypothesis A: 2 RED → GREEN (java + scala_deserialization_positive); cpp remains RED, deferred

milestones.title: CHANGELOG entry + local tag

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per vuln-source-parity-v1 M6 precedent
sections: ### Changed: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast now descends through nested constructor / call / instance / binary nodes for Java / Scala / (Cpp if Hypothesis B). BFS-over-named-descendants with bounded recursion (depth 5) and string-kind filter at every level.

### Closed-carry-forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B (java + scala_deserialization_positive; +cpp_deserialization_positive if Hypothesis B). vuln_migration_v1_red red count drops from 8 to 5 (or 6).

### Retained: closes-#24 string-literal regression-guard preserved at every recursion level; existing direct-identifier path for non-descend-through first-args unchanged; helper invariants (identifier validation, string-kind filter, is_named() filter).

### Architectural note: helper extension only; NO public API change; NO new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants; NO new bank entries; sub-helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent mirrors the BFS pattern previously used for PHP echo_statement (taint.rs:3954-3982) and OCaml application_expression (taint.rs:3989-4016).

stop_thresholds: CHANGELOG.md entry written and committed
Local git tag var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 applied
NO push, NO publish, NO version bump
git tag --list var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 returns the tag

loc_delta_summary.note: Single-function extension in single source file. Doc-comment block is ~10-20 LOC of the 60. M3 verification is reports-only (no source change). All numbers approximate; executor sees true delta in git diff.

extended_by: vuln-migration-v1 M3 (PHP echo_statement / print_intrinsic special-case L3941-3983)
invocation_sites: taint.rs:4316 (detect_sinks_ast — fallback after regex_patterns extract_call_arg / extract_sink_var_from_statement)
investigation_findings.current_helper_root_cause_for_3_fixtures: Helper does NOT descend through nested constructor / call / instance first-args. When first named arg-list child is itself object_creation_expression (Java) / call_expression (Scala) / etc, node_text(child).split('.').next() yields fragments like 'new java' which fail is_valid_identifier — helper returns None — sink dropped.
investigation_findings.java_fixture_node_kind_chain: object_creation_expression(outer) → arguments → object_creation_expression(inner) → arguments → method_invocation('d.getBytes()') → text='d.getBytes()' → split('.').next()='d' → valid
investigation_findings.scala_fixture_node_kind_chain_HYPOTHESIS: call_expression(outer) → arguments → call_expression(inner; instance_expression-wrapped) → arguments → method_invocation_or_field_expression('d.getBytes') → text='d.getBytes' → split('.').next()='d' → valid. M1 inspection pins down exact node kinds at pinned grammar.

cpp_fixture_node_kind_HYPOTHESES.A: declaration with function_declarator — descendant doesn't match call_node_kinds(Cpp); helper not invoked; sink also doesn't fire (per M2-report L91 empirical sinks=[])
cpp_fixture_node_kind_HYPOTHESES.B: call_expression with first arg binary_expression('std::stringstream(d) >> obj') — descend into binary_expression's left → call_expression('std::stringstream(d)') → arguments → identifier('d') → valid

investigation_findings.string_literal_regression_guard_preserva: string_node_kinds(language) filter applied at every recursion level. Existing FP fixtures (cpp/java/scala/deserialization_string_literal_fp.{ext}) wrap constructor names in outer string-literals at the assignment level; caught by is_in_string at descendant filter (taint.rs:4262) upstream of helper invocation. Internal string-literal in constructor arg covered by per-level string_kinds skip.
investigation_findings.recursion_depth_5_rationale: Real-world chains rarely exceed 3 levels. Bound prevents pathological deep-template/generic recursion. Tunable upward if a real-world fixture surfaces.
investigation_findings.regression_test_strategy: M3 sweep covers vuln_migration_v1_red (166 tests) + vuln_migration_v1_composite_red (1) + rr_framework_integ_test (18) + test_e2e_in_security_vuln (36 test_e2e_*) + binary smoke on 18 *_string_literal_fp fixtures. No new test fixtures authored — the 3 existing RED tests ARE the contract.

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: cpp Hypothesis A surfaces — helper extension does NOT close cpp_deserialization_positive
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: M1 resolves before M2; if A, scope drops to 2/3 + cpp deferred to cpp-deser-declaration-v1 follow-on

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Recursive descent picks up wrong identifier (e.g., 'getBytes' instead of 'd')
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: BFS prefers leftmost source-order leaf; method_invocation text 'd.getBytes()' correctly splits to 'd' via existing identifier-extraction logic

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Descent regresses C fgets-style sources or 158-GREEN test surface
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Descent triggers ONLY when first child kind ∈ descend-through set; M3 GREEN-regression sweep is gating

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: string_kinds filter not applied at recursion leaves causes closes-#24 regression
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Filter explicitly applied at every BFS step per design; M3 binary-smoke on 18 FP fixtures is gating
M1 captures actual node kinds at HEAD's pinned grammars; helper comment cites them

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Recursion depth bound too low for valid-but-deep chains
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Bound conservative (5); falls through to existing fallback chain on overflow; tunable
Only included if Hypothesis B; M3 sweep confirms no cpp GREEN→RED

HypothesisB_outcome.remaining_buckets: Bucket A only (1 Ruby + 4 Rust); Bucket B fully closed

HypothesisA_outcome.remaining_buckets: Bucket A (5: 1 Ruby + 4 Rust) + Bucket B partial (1 cpp_deserialization_positive)
HypothesisA_outcome.rationale: 1 over original cap of 5; explicit non-additive-resolution with documented follow-on milestone cpp-deser-declaration-v1
